comments.author: @JerriHådding
comments.text: I am a 76 year old woman originally from San Diego, California, who has lived in Sweden with my Swedish husband for the last 28 years.  I enjoyed your video and will be sharing it with family and friends in California and Germany.  There WERE inaccuracies in your narrative, but even they were shared with such charm that I will only acknowledge that they exist.  Thank you for sharing this extraordinary country with the world!

comments.author: @Nels921
comments.text: This is a pice of the country, missing from some of the real parts forest, farmers, Småland, the 1000 small lakes, every part in Sweden are for free people.

comments.author: @kronop8884
comments.text:
People don't leave the office to have a cup of coffee, there is typically some sort of coffee break area in the office where people gather for a 15 minute coffee break.
Meatballs where a thing long before Charles XII but back in the 1600s it was called "frikadeller", anyway it didn't become a staple food until the 1800s
Anyway there are no sources whatsoever that connect meatballs to Charles XII or anyone in his entourage

replies.author: @roymackay9801
replies.text: Robot voices almost always get local pronunciations wrong e.g it's pronounced 'LAAGOM' not 'LAGGOM' but YOU must also be careful because when you write in English you should have spelled it ( haven't ) in your sentence and also it should have been ( research ) in present form. Just trying to help after being born in Scotland but also lived in Sweden for over 45 years. Ha en bra dag, kompis!
